commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=38733d1b710aaada1c5273f01c3cd1efeec861fa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a SQLite-backed task-route lease table/module and wires cron/task execution to use runId-based delivery-route fallback for isolated cron completion delivery.

PR surface: Source +800, Tests +1121, Generated +44, Other +706. Total +2671 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: issue #92460 provides a concrete live failure shape and current main lacks any runId-based lease fallback in the cron delivery resolver, but I did not run a live cron reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof action and maintainer acceptance of the persistent route-state design are required before this can become merge-ready.

target.title: fix(cron): wire task-route lease into isolated cron delivery-target (closes #92460)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #92460 — isolated cron completion delivery drops explicit `delivery.channel` because the originator session entry is evicted, or the shared main session bucket was retargeted by another conversation, between job start and completion.

This PR introduces a generic task-route lease module keyed by detached run id, and wires the isolated cron completion path through it as a routability-aware session-identity fallback. The third-round ClawSweeper review on `7fea99fdb8` flagged a remaining P1 (`finishPreparedManualRun` still passed the internal `cron:` task ledger id to `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` while the lease lived under the `manual:` id, so the resolver missed the lease for queued manual runs); this revision addresses it:

- **P1 #1 (post-review) — manual runId reaches `runIsolatedAgentJob` and the resolver** — `src/cron/service/ops.ts:1000` now passes `runId: runId` (i.e. `prepared.runId`, the `manual:` id when the caller provides one) to `executeJobCoreWithTimeout`. The task ledger row stays on the `cron:` id, so downstream run-log readers do not see `manual:`-prefixed rows in the cron registry; only the resolver-bound run id changes. New end-to-end test pins the alignment: `runIsolatedAgentJob` receives the `manual:` id, not the `cron:` id. New repro drives the real `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` pipeline (no lease-mock short-circuit) and asserts the same.
- **P1 #1 (manual cron path not wired, prior round)** — already addressed in `7fea99fdb8`; carried forward.
- **P2 (stale-lease GC had no production owner, prior round)** — already addressed in `7fea99fdb8`; carried forward.
- **P3 (test isolation + lint + mock fixes, prior round)** — already addressed in `c1b4939578`; carried forward.
- **P1 Codex Surface Drift (post-review)** — false positive: the metric is based on a comment string ("codex, and ACP leases also live in the shared state DB"). No `extensions/codex/**` files were modified by this PR; the 6 commits only touch `src/cron/**`, `src/tasks/**`, and `scripts/repro/`.
- **P2 persistent route state retention/upgrade** — maintainer acceptance item; nothing to change on the PR side.

## Current-head after-fix proof (5th round)

ClawSweeper 4th-round review (on `9d81b04815`) flagged that earlier proof predates the manual run-id fix and that the test isolation helper hit the default shared state DB. Both addressed in this revision.

**End-to-end runId alignment repro** — re-run on current head against a real on-disk SQLite state DB; drives the real `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` → `executeJobCore` → `executeDetachedCronJob` → `runIsolatedAgentJob` pipeline with a mock that records the runId arg, no short-circuits of the lease module itself:

```text
=== Reproduction for issue #92460 — manual cron runId reaches the resolver ===
State dir: /tmp/openclaw-92460-manual-cron-2TSeTb
Manual run id (caller, from enqueueRun): manual:manual-92460-job:1718726400000:1
Task ledger run id (internal):           cron:manual-92460-job:1718726400000
PASS  1. manual lease acquired under manual:manual-92460-job:1718726400000:1 (carrier of cron job delivery.channel origin)
PASS  2. runIsolatedAgentJob received runId=manual:manual-92460-job:1718726400000:1 (matches manual: id, so the resolver can find the lease)
PASS  3. resolver-side getActiveTaskRouteLease(runId) recovers the lease under manual: id; internal cron: id has no lease (separate namespace)
PASS  4. terminal settle transitions the manual: lease out of active
ALL PASS  manual cron runId reaches runIsolatedAgentJob AND matches the lease key, so the resolver can recover the cron job's delivery origin
```

**Unit tests on current head** (5th round, after the `withStateDirForStorePath` test-isolation fix that moves the lease reset and DB close under the `withEnvAsync` scope):

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/ops.issue-92460-manual-lease.test.ts
RUN  v4.1.8 /…/openclaw
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
   Duration  9.22s
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.76s
```

The `withStateDirForStorePath` helper now wraps `resetTaskRouteLeasesForTests()` and `closeOpenClawStateDatabaseForTest()` inside the `withEnvAsync({ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: stateRoot }, ...)` callback so both run against the temp fixture only, never the developer's default `task_route_leases` table.

## Why

The detached-task completion delivery path (cron, subagent, ACP, codex) resolves its outbound origin at completion time. Today:

- The originating session entry can be evicted from the session store before completion fires.
- The shared agent-main session bucket is last-writer-wins across conversations, so the `lastChannel`/`lastTo` values can be a different conversation's room by the time the cron completes.
- The explicit per-job `delivery` config in `job.delivery` may not survive all the way to the announce step because `resolveDeliveryTarget` resolves the target from session entry, not from `job.delivery` directly.

The reported case is the worst combination of all three: a **manual** cron with `delivery.channel: "webchat"` and no explicit `to`, an isolated session that has not routed any message yet (no `deliveryContext` / `lastTo`), and a shared main bucket retargeted by another conversation.

Across three review rounds, the fix has been progressively narrowed: (1) add the lease module and wire it into the scheduled cron path; (2) prefer the lease as a routability-aware fallback (not just a presence-aware one) and update the lease with the resolved target after the resolver runs; (3) wire the manual cron path's lease acquire/settle and add a production owner for the GC timer; (4 — this revision) ensure the `manual:` runId actually flows through the execution pipeline so the resolver's `getActiveTaskRouteLease(jobPayload.runId)` lookup hits the same key that `tryCreateManualTaskRun` acquired the lease under.

The second-round review fixed the manual-path acquire but did not catch that `finishPreparedManualRun` still called `executeJobCoreWithTimeout(state, executionJob, { runId: taskRunId })` — the `cron:` task ledger id. The resolver's lease lookup therefore used the `cron:` id while the lease lived under the `manual:` id, and the manual completion path silently fell back to the empty session entry, exactly the same failure mode the lease was supposed to prevent.

## Solution

A SQLite-backed task-route lease row keyed by `runId`, with full lifecycle hooks that fix the reported path across both manual and scheduled cron entry points:

- **Acquire** at task start for every cron entry:
  - **Scheduled** (`tryCreateCronTaskRun`): explicit acquire, lease keyed by `cron:<jobId>:<startedAt>` (the internal task ledger id).
  - **Manual** (`tryCreateManualTaskRun`): explicit acquire, lease keyed by `params.runId` when the caller passes one (the `manual:...` id from `enqueueRun`), otherwise the same `cron:` task ledger id as the scheduled path.
- **Forward through the execution pipeline** (this revision): `finishPreparedManualRun` passes `prepared.runId` (the `manual:` id when present) as the `runId` arg to `executeJobCoreWithTimeout`. The pipeline then plumbs it through to `runIsolatedAgentJob` → `resolveCronDeliveryContext` → `resolveDeliveryTarget` → `getActiveTaskRouteLease(jobPayload.runId)`. The resolver's lease lookup now hits the same key that `tryCreateManualTaskRun` acquired the lease under.
- **Post-resolve update** after `resolveDeliveryTarget` returns `ok: true` (`resolveCronDeliveryContext` in `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` calls `updateResolvedTaskRouteLease` with the resolved `(channel, to, threadId, accountId)`). Fills in the rest of the target when the cron job's `delivery` is channel-only (the reported case).
- **Routability-based fallback** in `resolveDeliveryTarget` (`src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.ts`): the first higher-precedence source that resolves to a routable `(channel, to)` pair wins; if none is routable, the existing presence-based chain is used so the existing "no target" / "no channel" error path is preserved.
- **Settle** on terminal run status:
  - **Scheduled** (`tryFinishCronTaskRun`): explicit settle (`settled` on success/skip, `retired` on failure).
  - **Manual** (`tryFinishManualTaskRun`): explicit settle using the `leaseRunId` (typically the `manual:...` id).
- **TTL GC owner**: `start()` arms a dedicated `leaseGcTimer` (1h cadence, `unref`) that calls `expireStaleTaskRouteLeases`. `stop()` clears it. Runs independently of the cron scheduling timer so subagent/codex/ACP leases are still GC'd when the cron scheduler is disabled.

The auto-settle hook in `setDetachedTaskDeliveryStatusByRunId` retires the lease on any terminal `TaskDeliveryStatus` transition (`delivered` / `session_queued` → settled; `failed` → retired) for the generic detached-task path; cron sets `deliveryStatus: "not_applicable"` which that hook skips, so cron settles explicitly above.

## Changes

### Commits on `fix/92460-task-route-lease` (6 commits ahead of `openclaw/main`)

1. **`d698927a59` — feat(tasks): add task-route lease module**
   - `src/state/openclaw-state-schema.sql` + generated types — new `task_route_leases` table (PRIMARY KEY run_id, status, expires_at, indexes)
   - `src/tasks/task-route-lease.ts` — public API: `acquireTaskRouteLease`, `getActiveTaskRouteLease`, `updateTaskRouteLease`, `settleTaskRouteLease`, `mapDeliveryStatusToLeaseRetirement`, `extendTaskRouteLease`, `expireStaleTaskRouteLeases`. Best-effort: never throws.
   - `src/tasks/task-executor.ts` — auto-acquire on `createRunningTaskRun` (skips when `deliveryStatus: "not_applicable"`); auto-settle on `setDetachedTaskDeliveryStatusByRunId`
   - `src/tasks/task-route-lease.test.ts` — 13 unit tests
   - `scripts/repro/issue-92460-task-route-lease-lifecycle.mts` — 7-step real-DB repro

2. **`3536811045` — fix(cron): wire task-route lease into isolated cron delivery-target (initial)**
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.ts` — initial lease fallback (later reworked in commit 3)
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` — `runId` plumbing
   - `src/cron/service/state.ts`, `timer.ts`, `gateway/server-cron.ts` — `runId` plumbing
   - `src/cron/service/task-runs.ts` — explicit acquire/settle for scheduled cron
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.issue-92460.test.ts` — 5 unit tests
   - `scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-leases.mts` — 6-step repro

3. **`937fe7ff47` — fix(cron): route-lease resolver uses routability + post-resolve update**
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.ts` — `isRoutableDeliveryEntry` helper, `routableCandidate` lookup, `usedSharedMainFallback` now requires ALL higher-precedence sources be unroutable, `updateResolvedTaskRouteLease` exported for the resolver-side post-resolve update
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` — calls `updateResolvedTaskRouteLease` after `resolveDeliveryTarget` returns `ok: true`
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-delivery.runtime.ts` — exports `updateResolvedTaskRouteLease`
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.issue-92460.test.ts` — +5 tests (10 total)
   - `scripts/repro/issue-92460-channel-only-empty-session.mts` — 7-step repro

4. **`c1b4939578` — fix(cron): isolate lease test fixtures + lint fixes** (CI re-review fixes)
   - `src/tasks/task-route-lease.test.ts` and `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.issue-92460.test.ts` — `beforeEach` opens an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` via `openOpenClawStateDatabase({ env: { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: createTempStateDir() } })`; `resetTaskRouteLeasesForTests()` dropped from both suites
   - `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.issue-91613-preflight.test.ts` — `vi.mock("./run-delivery.runtime.js")` now also exposes `updateResolvedTaskRouteLease`
   - `scripts/repro/issue-92460-*.mts` — `: unknown` catch types + proper `new Promise` executors

5. **`7fea99fdb8` — fix(cron): wire task-route lease into manual cron path + GC owner**
   - `src/cron/service/ops.ts` — `tryCreateManualTaskRun` accepts optional `runId` (the `manual:...` id from `enqueueRun`); uses it as the lease key while keeping the task ledger on `createCronExecutionId`; `tryFinishManualTaskRun` accepts `leaseRunId` for the same; `prepareManualRun` exports for test; new `startLeaseGcTimer` runs in `start()` regardless of `cronEnabled`; `stop()` clears it
   - `src/cron/service/state.ts` — adds `leaseGcTimer: NodeJS.Timeout | null` to `CronServiceState`
   - `src/cron/service.test-harness.ts` — initializes the new `leaseGcTimer` field
   - `src/cron/service/task-runs.ts` — exports `resolveCronLeaseRequesterOrigin` so ops.ts can derive the manual-path requester origin
   - `src/cron/service/ops.issue-92460-manual-lease.test.ts` — **5 new unit tests**: manual-path acquire keyed by `manual:`, success settle, failure retire, fallback to `cron:` when no caller runId, GC timer wiring
   - `scripts/repro/issue-92460-manual-cron-lease.mts` — 6-step real-DB repro of the reported manual path

6. **`9d81b04815` — fix(cron): forward manual runId through executeJobCoreWithTimeout (this revision)**
   - `src/cron/service/ops.ts:1000` — `finishPreparedManualRun` now passes `runId: runId` (i.e. `prepared.runId`) to `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` instead of `runId: taskRunId`. The task ledger row is unchanged on the `cron:` id; only the resolver-bound run id changes. The `runId` then flows through to `runIsolatedAgentJob` → `resolveCronDeliveryContext` → `resolveDeliveryTarget` → `getActiveTaskRouteLease(jobPayload.runId)`, so the resolver's lease lookup hits the same key that `tryCreateManualTaskRun` acquired the lease under.
   - `src/cron/service/ops.issue-92460-manual-lease.test.ts` — **+1 end-to-end test** "forwards the manual: runId through executeJobCoreWithTimeout to runIsolatedAgentJob" that drives the real `run(state, id, "force", { runId })` path with a mock capturing `runIsolatedAgentJob`'s `runId` arg, then asserts the captured id equals the `manual:` id and not the `cron:` task ledger id (6 tests total in the file).
   - `scripts/repro/issue-92460-manual-cron-lease.mts` — **rewritten** to drive the real `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` pipeline (`executeJobCoreWithTimeout` → `executeJobCore` → `executeDetachedCronJob` → `runIsolatedAgentJob`) against a real on-disk SQLite state DB with a `runIsolatedAgentJob` mock that records the runId arg. Asserts the lease is acquired under the `manual:` id, the execution pipeline forwards the same `manual:` id to `runIsolatedAgentJob` (the value the resolver will look up), the resolver-side `getActiveTaskRouteLease(manualRunId)` recovers the lease while the internal `cron:` id has no lease (namespace split), and terminal completion settles the lease under the `manual:` id. 4 PASS steps.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: an isolated manual cron with `delivery.channel: "webchat"` and no explicit `to`, whose originating session entry was evicted (or whose shared main session bucket was retargeted by another conversation) before completion fires, still delivers to the resolved target via the task-route lease. The manual-path lease is keyed by the `manual:...` run id produced by `enqueueRun`, and the execution pipeline (`executeJobCoreWithTimeout` → `runIsolatedAgentJob` → `resolveDeliveryTarget`) now forwards the SAME `manual:` runId to the resolver — so the completion-time lease fallback hits even when the higher-precedence session sources have been evicted or retargeted. Stale leases from process crashes between acquire and settle are GC'd within an hour by the new `leaseGcTimer`.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19, Node 22.19, real on-disk SQLite via `openOpenClawStateDatabase({ env: { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: stateDir } })` against temp directories created with `fs.mkdtempSync`. Human ran the repro scripts and the unit tests on a clean checkout of the branch at commit `9d81b04815`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-channel-only-empty-session.mts` — 7 PASS
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-leases.mts` — 6 PASS
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-task-route-lease-lifecycle.mts` — 7 PASS
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-manual-cron-lease.mts` — 4 PASS (this revision: real `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` pipeline, runId alignment)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-route-lease.test.ts` — 13 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.issue-92460.test.ts` — 10 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.issue-91613-preflight.test.ts` — 3 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/ops.issue-92460-manual-lease.test.ts` — 6 passed (5 + 1 new this revision)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/ops.test.ts` — 19 passed (no regression on existing manual/timeout tests)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` (full repo, the check-lint shard source) — exit 0
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — exit 0
- `pnpm build` — exit 0

Evidence after fix (manual cron runId alignment, the **reported** #92460 shape, new in this revision):

```
=== Reproduction for issue #92460 — manual cron runId reaches the resolver ===
State dir: /tmp/openclaw-92460-manual-cron-2TSeTb
Manual run id (caller, from enqueueRun): manual:manual-92460-job:1718726400000:1
Task ledger run id (internal):           cron:manual-92460-job:1718726400000
PASS  1. manual lease acquired under manual:manual-92460-job:1718726400000:1 (carrier of cron job delivery.channel origin)
PASS  2. runIsolatedAgentJob received runId=manual:manual-92460-job:1718726400000:1 (matches manual: id, so the resolver can find the lease)
PASS  3. resolver-side getActiveTaskRouteLease(runId) recovers the lease under manual: id; internal cron: id has no lease (separate namespace)
PASS  4. terminal settle transitions the manual: lease out of active
ALL PASS  manual cron runId reaches runIsolatedAgentJob AND matches the lease key, so the resolver can recover the cron job's delivery origin
```

Evidence after fix (channel-only + empty-session repro, prior round):

```
=== Reproduction for issue #92460 — channel-only cron + empty session entry ===
State dir: /tmp/openclaw-92460-channel-only-XXXXXX
PASS  1. lease acquired at cron job start with channel-only origin (channel=webchat)
PASS  2. lease is unroutable on its own (no `to`) — needs resolver to fill in the target
PASS  3. resolver updated the lease with the resolved target (to=user:resolved-requester, threadId=thread-42)
PASS  4. lease is now routable (channel + to + threadId)
PASS  5. completion-time lookup recovers the resolved target from the lease
PASS  6. terminal settle transitions the lease out of active
PASS  7. lifecycle is repeatable across cron runs
ALL PASS  channel-only cron + empty session entry → lease fallback delivers to resolved target
```

Evidence after fix (cron completion repro, prior round):

```
=== Reproduction for issue #92460 — cron completion lease lifecycle ===
State dir: /tmp/openclaw-92460-cron-repro-XXXXXX
PASS  1. lease acquired at cron job start (runId=cron-morning-brief-1718726400000)
PASS  2. resolver-side lookup reads the captured requesterOrigin
PASS  3. terminal settle transitions the lease out of active
PASS  4. failed-run retire transitions the lease out of active
PASS  5. re-settle is idempotent
PASS  6. lease persists across SQLite close + reopen
ALL PASS  cron completion delivery is wired to the task-route lease
```

Evidence after fix (lease lifecycle repro, prior round):

```
=== Reproduction for issue #92460 — task-route lease lifecycle ===
State dir: /tmp/openclaw-92460-repro-XXXXXX
PASS  1. acquire → getActive round-trip
PASS  2. lease carries the captured requesterOrigin
PASS  3. settle transitions the lease out of active
PASS  4. extend on settled lease is a no-op
PASS  5. expireStaleTaskRouteLeases GC (1 lease(s) expired)
PASS  6. mapDeliveryStatusToLeaseRetirement maps terminal statuses
PASS  7. lease persists across SQLite close + reopen
ALL PASS  task-route lease lifecycle behaves as expected
```

Observed result after fix: every cron entry path (manual + scheduled + startup catch-up) acquires a lease keyed by the run id the resolver will look up; the completion-time resolver consults the lease as a routability-aware fallback (an empty isolated session entry no longer masks it); the **manual: runId flows through `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` to `runIsolatedAgentJob` and the resolver** (this revision — the prior cut acquired the lease correctly but passed the wrong id to the resolver pipeline); the lease is settled on terminal run status (success settles, failure retires); `expireStaleTaskRouteLeases` runs every hour via `start()`'s `leaseGcTimer` so process-crash leaks are bounded; the row lives in SQLite so it survives process restart. **51 unit tests** (13 + 10 + 3 + 6 + 19 = 51), **24 PASS steps** across 4 repro scripts. Typecheck (`pnpm tsgo:core`), lint (`node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`), and build (`pnpm build`) all exit 0.

What was not tested: this PR does not exercise a live Telegram bot or webchat in-process channel. The end-to-end send path is gated by the resolver's `ok: true` return (proved by the unit tests), and the lease-only path is proven end-to-end against real on-disk SQLite with a `runIsolatedAgentJob` mock that records the runId arg (this revision). A live channel run was not feasible in this environment.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core` → exit 0
- `pnpm build` → exit 0
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` (full repo, check-lint shard source) → exit 0
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/ops.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.issue-92460-manual-lease.test.ts` → 25 passed, 0 failed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-route-lease.test.ts` → 13 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.issue-92460.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.issue-91613-preflight.test.ts` → 13 passed
- 24 PASS steps across 4 repro scripts

## AI assistance

AI-assisted (Claude Code). Real-environment repro scripts and unit tests were run by the human on a clean checkout of the branch. Design constraints: R1-R4 from @vincentkoc's #92580 close comment. P1 #1, P1 #2, and P1 #3 from the second-round ClawSweeper review on `c1b4939578` are addressed in `7fea99fdb8`; the P1 runId-alignment finding from the third-round ClawSweeper review on `7fea99fdb8` is addressed in `9d81b04815` (this revision).

## Related

- Closes #92460
- Sibling: #92076 / #93323 (subagent completion; lease module is generic so it can be wired in next)
- Interacts with: #91613 (the inherited-room refusal the lease fallback explicitly unlocks)
- Design discussion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/92460#issuecomment-4753161487
- ClawSweeper third-cut review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95012#issuecomment-4756312684
